steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Since both expressions equal $y$, set them equal to each other:
$$4x = x^2 - 12$$

steps.title: Rearrange to standard form and factor
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side:
$$x^2 - 4x - 12 = 0$$

Factor by finding two numbers that multiply to $-12$ and add to $-4$. Those numbers are $-6$ and $2$:
$$(x - 6)(x + 2) = 0$$

This gives $x = 6$ or $x = -2$.

steps.title: Apply the positivity constraint
steps.content: Since the problem requires $x > 0$, we eliminate $x = -2$ and keep $x = 6$.
